description: In 2021, mobile operators like Grameenphone (Telenor subsidiary) in Bangladesh faced intense public backlash after the government imposed a supplementary duty on mobile services, which operators passed directly to consumers. Users campaigned against the price hike, viewing internet access as a essential right.

key_events.description: Government announces budget with increased duty on mobile services.
Mobile operators enforce price hike on data packages.
Viral social media protest #NoVATonInternet spreads across Bangladesh.
Telecom regulatory commission defends the tax policy despite protests.

calibration_notes: Shows the tension between state-mandated fiscal policy and corporate-consumer relationships.
